Greek-Entry.English-Entry: καὶ θυλάκιον, ἐν ᾧ οἱ ἡνίοχοι τὰς μάστιγας ἀποτίθενται
τὰ λαγαρὰ ἐσθίοντα, ὅ ἐστιν εὔθραυστα καὶ εὐτελῆ τινα
ὁ εὐθὺς λανθάνων τοῦ ἀγῶνος καὶ φόβου
to be the tutelary deity of
who have a post assigned to them by lot
have him assigned to one by lot
those on whom the lot fell
the hollow on each side below the ribs, flank
ἡ Ἀφροδίτη, ὅτι ἐπὶ τῷ ἐν Ἀρκαδίᾳ ποταμῷ Λάδωνι ἐγεννήθη
πέμματα, οἱ δὲ σπέρματα, ἱερὰ ἀπάργματα
animal"s skin with hair left on
ὕδωρ ἁλμυρὸν ἁλσὶ πεποιημένον, ὃ πίνουσιν οἱ τῶν Μακεδόνων ἀγροῖκοι
ἀρχή τις, ἔνθα οἱ κλέπται κρίνονται
ἐλάττω ἔχειν γῆν τὸν ἀγρὸν ἐπιστολῆς Λακωνικῆς
one who takes part with them
to be mad on Spartan ways
with stripes in Laconian fashion, Poxy.
τὰ χάσματα, ἢ οἱ μόνοι τῶν ἀνθρώπων, καὶ ἰχθῦς
take by violence, carry off as prize
take hold of, lay hold on
seize and keep hold of, obtain possession of
a fabulous monster said to feed on man"s flesh
to be handed on like a torch
carry the torch, run in the torch-race
kind of comet resembling a torch
take part in a torch-bearing procession
make oneself splendid, pride oneself on
that which is assigned by lot, an allotment of land
enrol in the class subject to poll-tax
τοὺς λαοὺς εἰς ὄπιν ἄγειν . . διὰ τῆς ἀλαζονείας
serving as a passage for the people, man-conveying
ἐκκενοῦν, ἀφ" οὗ καὶ τὸ ὄρυγμα
the soft part of the body
pleasant to the taste, dainty, sweet
pleasant to the ear, sweet to hear
ἐν τοῖς ἀθροίσμασιν ἔλεγον, ὡς ἐπῳδῶν
οἱ περὶ τὰς Λίμνας χλωροὶ βάτραχοι
slave who had charge of the night-stool
deaf from hair growing in the ears
ὡς σοφιστοῦ τοῦ Λάσου καὶ πολυπλόκου

the great Nile perch, Perca (Lates) niloticus
the state of a hired labourer, service
service paid to the gods, worship
παραξιφίς, καὶ ἡ περὶ ζώνην μάχαιρα
the chips of stone in hewing,
the choreutae who stood in the middle

seller of booty, one who has bought up booty to retail
who took charge of the booty

sense do you say this? Ap.
do we mean to say? Grg.
a blooming face, a peacock"s tail
ἡ τελείως ὡς κόνις διαλελυμένη, λείως γὰρ τελείως
to be upon a smooth sea
to be left without, to be forsaken of
to be left behind in a race
to be left behind, be absent
come short of, be inferior to
you come short of, understand not

serve public offices at one"s own cost
perform public duties, serve the state
the officer who superintended the workmen, carpenters
subsidiary action springing out of a trial
who have quitted the light of day,
ὁ τελείως ἐκκεκαυμένους τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ἔχων
good at speaking, able to speak
with the force of a word
capable of being spoken, to be spoken
the fruit of marriage, a child
that which is peeled off, rind, husk
base in form of a lion,
in a chariot drawn by lions
bearing the figure of a lion

leprosy, which makes the skin scaly
as thin as thin can be
ἀπὸ τοῦ λέπους, καὶ τῆς χωρίσεως
to be contained in a husk
president of the council of Lesbos,
μεγάλην. ὑδρηλήν. Δίδυμος τὴν καταδυομένην εἰς πέλαγος πέτραν. οἱ δὲ τὴν νοτεράν. ἄλλοι δὲ σπίδα βαθεῖαν. οἱ δὲ λόχμην

the south wind which cleared the weather
clad in white down to the feet
a white spot in the eye
εἰ δ" ἂν λευτον μὲ ἰνφορβίε
grown with grass fit to make a bed
ὑπὸ Ἀργείων ἡ θυσία ἐπιτελουμένη τῇ Ἥρᾳ
one who has just given birth
the temple of the daughters of Leos
one of their own people, their fellow-citizen.

to be carried off as booty, to be won by force

one who declaims in a hollow voice

a humour that gathers in the corner of the eye, rheum
secretion of rheum in the eyes, PMed.
socket into which the mast fitted
the meeting-point of the sinuses of the dura mater

written complaint lodged with the Archon, as the first step in private actions
to be put in legal possession of an inheritance

one that divines from the smoke of frankincense
a wind between south-west and south
governor of region on the west bank of the Nile,
the west bank of the Nile,
cry out with a loud, clear voice

which vent themselves in shrill wailings

laying of a foundation of unworked stones

one who builds with stones picked out

part the grain from the chaff, winnow

on the margin of the lakes
candidate for the prize at the Lenaea
edible oyster, which was kept in ponds by the sea
one who starves himself from avarice,
treat by hunger, diet severely, starve

one who watches nets to see whether anything is caught
laying of nets: the nets laid,
to be surrounded and caught with nets

joint crop of flax and grass,
to be in want of men
with shining body, sleek of skin

desert, refuse to serve in arms

to be eager for, long for

of a plain way of life

the string struck with the forefinger

as nearly in words as possible

practical arithmetic, the art of arithmetic
skilled in tricking out a speech
to be apprehended by the intellect alone
to have a passion for study
to be furnished with a point
one who is in arrear with taxes,
allow to remain in arrear, carry over

the outer part of a split piece of leather

put them in order of battle
the men that form the ambush
any armed band, body of troops

with pain, so as to feel

with a patched cloak, Com. Adesp.
made of the flowers of Nymphaea Nelumbo
